# UC-19.2.24 · HCI Deduplication Efficiency Ratio

## Description

Cluster-wide dedupe/compression ratio (Nutanix vs vSAN) vs baseline — efficiency regression indicates new noisy workloads or mis-tuned containers.

## Value

Cluster-wide dedupe/compression ratio (Nutanix vs vSAN) vs baseline — efficiency regression indicates new noisy workloads or mis-tuned containers.

## Implementation

Define `baseline_ratio` from lookup or 30-day rolling mean. Alert on >15% drop week-over-week.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-nutanix`, vSAN capacity.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `hci:storage_efficiency`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Define `baseline_ratio` from lookup or 30-day rolling mean. Alert on >15% drop week-over-week.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="hci:storage_efficiency" earliest=-24h
| eval ratio=logical_tb/physical_tb
| lookup hci_efficiency_baseline.csv cluster OUTPUT baseline_ratio
| where ratio < baseline_ratio*0.85
| stats latest(ratio) as r, latest(baseline_ratio) as baseline by cluster
| table cluster, r, baseline
```

Understanding this SPL

**HCI Deduplication Efficiency Ratio** — Cluster-wide dedupe/compression ratio (Nutanix vs vSAN) vs baseline — efficiency regression indicates new noisy workloads or mis-tuned containers.

Documented **Data sources**: `hci:storage_efficiency`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-nutanix`, vSAN capacity. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: hci:storage_efficiency. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="hci:storage_efficiency", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where ratio < baseline_ratio*0.85` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **HCI Deduplication Efficiency Ratio**): table cluster, r, baseline


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the same scope in the vendor HCI or compute console (UCS, vCenter, Nutanix Prism, and so on).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (dedupe ratio trend), Single value (fleet average), Table (regressions).

## SPL

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="hci:storage_efficiency" earliest=-24h
| eval ratio=logical_tb/physical_tb
| lookup hci_efficiency_baseline.csv cluster OUTPUT baseline_ratio
| where ratio < baseline_ratio*0.85
| stats latest(ratio) as r, latest(baseline_ratio) as baseline by cluster
| table cluster, r, baseline
```

## Visualization

Line chart (dedupe ratio trend), Single value (fleet average), Table (regressions).
